title: Proposed notice for 4 CCR 723-3
summary: We find that the record does not support changes proposed by the participants or included in the Recommended Decision, and we set aside the rules proposed in the Recommended Decision. The Commission shall issue a new NOPR to determine what pollution control projects are in the ordinary course of business. The Recommended Decision shall not become a decision of the Commission pursuant to 40-6-109, C.R.S.
source_evidence: Department of Regulatory Agencies Public Utilities Commission 4 CCR 723-3 2013-01184 11/12/2014 We find that the record does not support changes proposed by the participants or included in the Recommended Decision, and we set aside the rules proposed in the Recommended Decision. The Commission shall issue a new NOPR to determine what pollution control projects are in the ordinary course of business. The Recommended Decision shall not become a decision of the Commission pursuant to 40-6-109, C.R.S.

title: Proposed notice for 1 CCR 205-1
summary: This rulemaking is being terminated due to an overwhelming response from interested persons on all sides of the issues, many of whom raised significant concerns about the proposed changes to the required disclosure rules. Additionally, there are concerns that the hearing procedure issues have not been fully evaluated by interested persons.
source_evidence: Department of Revenue Motor Vehicle Dealer Board 1 CCR 205-1 2014-00929 10/16/2014 This rulemaking is being terminated due to an overwhelming response from interested persons on all sides of the issues, many of whom raised significant concerns about the proposed changes to the required disclosure rules. Additionally, there are concerns that the hearing procedure issues have not been fully evaluated by interested persons.

title: Proposed notice for 8 CCR 1201-18
summary: Based on the testimony received at that hearing it is evident that more discussion is needed among stakeholders. The Department has decided to withdraw the proposed amendments at this time. The Department may conduct a new hearing process in the future if the agency determines that rulemaking is necessary and if the various stakeholder groups are more aligned and in agreement with proposed amendments.
source_evidence: Department of Agriculture Animal Health Division 8 CCR 1201-18 2014-00982 11/03/2014 Based on the testimony received at that hearing it is evident that more discussion is needed among stakeholders. The Department has decided to withdraw the proposed amendments at this time. The Department may conduct a new hearing process in the future if the agency determines that rulemaking is necessary and if the various stakeholder groups are more aligned and in agreement with proposed amendments.
